title: Wireguard VPN
text: Wireguard tunnel runs on the wg0 interface on the router. Peers exchange public keys and complete a handshake before traffic flows. AllowedIPs decides which subnets route through the tunnel.

title: Caddy Reverse Proxy
text: Caddy is a reverse proxy that provisions automatic HTTPS with Let's Encrypt TLS certificates. Each site block maps a hostname to an upstream backend and renews certificates on its own.

title: AdGuard Home
text: AdGuard Home blocks ads by filtering DNS queries against blocklists. It acts as the upstream DNS resolver for the whole LAN and can rewrite hostnames for local services.

title: TrueNAS ZFS Snapshots
text: On TrueNAS a ZFS dataset can be snapshotted on a schedule and replicated to another pool. Snapshots are copy-on-write, so they cost almost nothing until data changes.

title: Docker Compose
text: A docker compose file declares services, volumes, and networks in one YAML document. Bring the whole stack up with compose up and each service gets a DNS name on the shared network.

title: Sourdough Bread
text: Sourdough needs an active starter fed with flour and water. Higher hydration dough is stickier but opens a more airy crumb. Bake in a preheated dutch oven for oven spring.

title: Pizza Dough
text: Pizza dough is flour, water, salt, and a little yeast left to rise slowly overnight in the fridge. Stretch by hand and bake as hot as the oven will go on a steel.

title: Coffee Brewing
text: For espresso use a fine grind and a fresh dose of beans; for pour-over go coarser and pour in slow circles. Dial in the grind until the shot runs in the right time.

title: Python asyncio
text: Python asyncio schedules coroutines on an event loop. You await a coroutine to suspend until it resolves, and gather runs many concurrently without threads.

title: Rust Ownership
text: Rust's borrow checker enforces ownership and lifetimes at compile time. A value has a single owner, and references must not outlive the data they borrow.

title: Kubernetes Cluster
text: A Kubernetes cluster schedules pods across worker nodes. A deployment declares the desired replica count and the control plane reconciles the actual state toward it. Services give pods a stable virtual IP and DNS name, while an ingress routes external HTTP traffic to the right service by hostname and path. ConfigMaps and secrets inject configuration into containers at runtime. Persistent volume claims bind storage to pods so data survives a reschedule. The scheduler places pods by resource requests and node affinity, and the kubelet on each node keeps the containers running and reports health back to the API server. Horizontal pod autoscalers add replicas when CPU or custom metrics cross a threshold, and rolling updates replace pods a few at a time so the service stays available during a deploy.

title: Guitar Practice
text: Guitar practice: warm up with scales, then drill chord changes with a metronome. Spend time on the fretboard learning where the notes sit so improvising gets easier.

title: Running Training
text: Marathon training mixes easy miles with interval sessions to build pace. Keep most runs slow and add one hard tempo run a week, tapering before race day.

title: Home Budget
text: The monthly budget tracks expenses against income in a spreadsheet, sets aside savings first, and flags any category that runs over its planned amount.

title: Book Notes: Deep Work
text: Deep Work by Cal Newport argues that focused, distraction-free attention is a rare skill. Schedule blocks for concentration and treat shallow email work as the exception.

title: Garden Tomatoes
text: Tomatoes want full sun, rich soil, and steady watering. Prune the suckers so the plant puts energy into fruit, and stake the vines before they sprawl.
